workItems.title: Fix repo environment friction and harden operator memory workflow

reviews.notes: Independent review found two regressions: numeric bare database names can be misread as stale-day overrides in memory audit, and the built-in workspace search fallback does not handle file roots or missing roots safely.

This work is repo-local rather than time-sensitive external research. I rechecked current repo state, memory health, and live policy alignment and found no external-source dependency that required new internet research for this change.

Reviewed the new repo-owned workspace search path, the CLI argument parsing changes, and the memory-health additions. Independent review initially found two edge-case regressions, and both were fixed with added coverage before this pass.

QA passed with the full test suite, memory audit, review-ledger check, workspace search spot checks, and operator brief verification. The new tests cover stale-memory surfacing, numeric bare database filenames, file-root search, and missing-root warnings.

Independent re-review passed after the edge-case fixes. The reviewer confirmed the numeric bare database-name regression is fixed for the intended relative-path case and that the built-in workspace search fallback now handles file roots and missing roots safely.

workItems.title: Automate GitHub quality gates and second-agent review defaults

reviews.notes: Checked current GitHub tooling and fixed the local GitHub command-line path, sign-in, and remote wiring.
Tightened review defaults, separated everyday build memory from the future world memory by default, and added a visible review ledger for GitHub to check.
Tests passed, the visible review record syncs cleanly, and the build-side memory now defaults to its own separate storage.

Checked the live GitHub repo and branch protection state on March 9, 2026. The repo is public, the repo-tracked GitHub policy now matches the live protection on main, and the policy intentionally uses pull-request gating plus required checks instead of manual GitHub approval clicks for the second-agent review signal.

Finished the GitHub automation layer by adding a repo-tracked branch protection policy script, a PR checklist enforcement workflow, package scripts for policy audit and apply, and contract and handoff updates that keep the GitHub path aligned with build-side governance.

npm test passed, npm run github:policy:audit reported matches true, npm run github:policy:apply reapplied the live protection cleanly, and the GitHub API now shows main protected with required checks review-record and verify.

Independent review passed. It confirmed the repo-tracked GitHub policy script works, the PR checklist is enforced in GitHub Actions, the existing verify check still validates tests and the review ledger, and the repo contract and handoff note both reflect the live protected-branch state. Residual risk: reviewer identity is still trusted through the review ledger rather than GitHub approval identity.
GitHub verify failed on March 9, 2026 because the brief test expected a specific steering label that did not reliably appear in the top brief output across environments.

Checked the live GitHub branch protection state on March 9, 2026 and confirmed the public repo still has the intended main-branch policy. The tracked policy script and the live repo settings still match, so this round is using current live state instead of stale assumptions.

The GitHub governance layer remains in place: the tracked policy script, the pull-request checklist enforcement workflow, the updated repo contract, and the less brittle brief test all hold together in the current branch state.

npm test passed with 28 passing tests and npm run github:policy:audit reported matches true for the live main-branch policy. That closes the earlier brittle-test failure with a fresh clean QA pass in round two.

Independent review found two blockers in round two: the current branch mixes GitHub automation with later reviewer-traceability work, so it is not a clean GitHub-automation-only review branch, and GitHub has not re-run verify on the current head yet because the open PR is still behind local HEAD. The GitHub automation files themselves still look coherent, but the branch scope and stale remote checks block a clean pass.

Checked the live GitHub state on March 9, 2026 after splitting the work onto a clean GitHub-automation-only branch. The new branch is pushed, GitHub sees it, and the live policy on main still matches the tracked policy file.

Kept the GitHub automation branch scoped to GitHub governance only and fixed the brittle brief test with a minimal change on that clean branch, without pulling the later reviewer-traceability work into this review round.

npm test passed locally on the clean GitHub-automation branch, npm run github:policy:audit still reported matches true, and GitHub push run 22878709358 finished with verify passing on the current branch head.

Fresh round-three independent review found no blocking issues. It confirmed the work is back on a dedicated GitHub-automation branch, the current head matches a passing GitHub verify run, and the actual GitHub automation pieces remain coherent. The stale progress note it flagged was corrected. Residual risk remains that the system still relies on the tracked review ledger instead of GitHub approval identity.

workItems.title: Harden independent reviewer identity and evidence

reviews.notes: Independent review found a blocker: revoking a reviewer later would retroactively invalidate already completed work in the ledger because exported historical reviews use the reviewer's current registry status instead of a status captured at review time. Residual risk also remains that reviewer identity is still trust-based rather than tool-verified.

This round did not depend on changing outside products or live platform behavior. I reviewed the local review-flow and migration path, and I kept the still-open dishonest-actor spoofing limit explicit instead of claiming it was solved.

Added a reviewer registry, stored review-time reviewer snapshots, backfilled historical reviewer identities including old UUID-style reviewer keys, and updated the command surface, ledger checks, tests, and repo contract to use the stricter traceability path.
npm test passed with 28 passing tests, npm run reviews:check passed, and the reviewer register and list commands worked locally while preserving older review history in exported ledger data.

Fresh round-two independent review found no blocking issues. It confirmed the revocation history regression is fixed, older reviewer identities including UUID-style keys are preserved in the ledger, and the remaining dishonest-actor spoofing gap stays explicitly open as a residual risk rather than being falsely claimed solved.

workItems.title: Add built-in dirty-workspace discipline and clean up current uncommitted build-side changes

reviews.notes: Checked the live repo state and current GitHub facts needed for the new public-repo cleanliness policy. This work did not depend on stale external assumptions; the key live fact was the repo now being public and therefore no longer carrying the old private-plan blocker.

Independent review passed. It confirmed the built-in workspace audit command exists, work completion now refuses a dirty repo, the repo contract and handoff note both mention the cleanliness rule, generated review artifacts are gone, and the remaining tree is a coherent build-governance package to commit together.

Reviewed the workspace-discipline changes for boundary drift. The build-side package now includes a built-in workspace audit command, clean-finish enforcement on work completion, contract updates, handoff updates, and test coverage without drifting into worldbuilding systems.

Full tests passed, loose generated review artifacts were removed, workspace audit reports only meaningful build-side changes, and the dirty-tree check now blocks work completion until the repo is actually clean.

workItems.title: Improve Notion mission-control readability and scanability

reviews.notes: Checked the live repo visibility and current GitHub plan docs on March 9, 2026 so the new public-repo state and the resolved private-plan blocker are grounded in current facts rather than stale assumptions.

Reviewed the Notion usability pass for clarity and boundary drift. The page now opens with a summary-first layout, the detail tables were narrowed to glanceable columns, the stale private-plan GitHub memory was resolved, and the work stayed strictly on the build side.

Verified the repo is public, the old private-plan GitHub blocker and steering are resolved, the Notion page now starts with a compact summary, the detail tables were narrowed, and operator memory audit is still clean with zero duplicate groups.

Independent review passed. It confirmed the repo is public, the stale private-plan GitHub memory was resolved, the Notion page now leads with a short summary, the detail tables were narrowed meaningfully, and the work stayed on the build side. Residual note: the page remains a hub with attached detail tables rather than one fully expanded inline board.

workItems.title: Wire Notion mission control for operator steerings, open problems, reviews, and active work

reviews.notes: Checked current GitHub docs and pricing before repeating the private-repo enforcement note. Protected branches are available in private repos on GitHub Pro, Team, and Enterprise Cloud, while stronger repository rules and push rules still depend on plan level, so the upgrade guidance remains directionally correct for a limited private setup.

Reviewed the mission-control wiring and handoff changes for boundary drift. The work stays on the build side, does not touch world-state systems, resolves the stale Notion visibility blocker, and records the remaining Notion inline-view limitation instead of hiding it.

Verified the canonical Notion page exists with the four build-side databases attached, confirmed the old Notion visibility blocker is no longer open in operator memory, ran the memory audit cleanly, and checked the updated handoff note.

Reviewed the mission-control wiring for boundary drift. The Notion setup stays strictly on the build side, mirrors active work, reviews, steerings, and open problems, resolves the stale Notion visibility blocker, and updates the handoff note without touching worldbuilding systems.

Checked current GitHub documentation and pricing on March 9, 2026. Protected branches and rulesets are available for private repositories on paid GitHub plans, and stronger repository rules are a Team-level path, so the upgrade note is directionally correct if this private setup is still below that tier.

Independent review found no blocking issues. It confirmed the canonical Notion page exists with the four build-side databases, the work stayed out of worldbuilding systems, and the handoff note matches the live state. Residual risk: the current Notion surface is a live hub page rather than one fully expanded inline board.

Verified the canonical Notion page exists, the four build-side databases were created and populated, the old Notion blocker is resolved, operator memory audit is clean with zero duplicate groups, and the operator brief now shows the new work plus the current open failures.

workItems.title: Harden operator memory and recursive review flow

reviews.notes: Checked current live GitHub documentation and pricing for private-repository enforcement limits, and confirmed the Notion connection is still not exposed in this session before treating it as available.

Hardened operator memory so repeated resolved items reopen cleanly, status changes refresh all affected search records, cancelled work stays out of the open brief, reopened items rise in the brief, and duplicate auditing is less noisy for numbered batches.
Ran the full test suite successfully, verified zero duplicate warnings in live operator memory, and checked the live operator brief after cleanup.

Independent review came back clean after two fix cycles. No findings remained. Residual risks are still trust-based separate-review identity, Notion not visible in this session, and GitHub private-plan enforcement limits.

workItems.title: Harden operator memory hygiene and recursive review flow
Current live research on memory and world orchestration
Bootstrap agent contract and review gates

reviews.notes: Added AGENTS-first repo contract plus enforceable work-item and audit tables, methods, and CLI commands.
Reviewed current 2026 patterns across OpenAI, OpenClaw, LangGraph, Mem0, and Letta before tightening the repo contract.
Ran npm test successfully after adding governance features and verified CLI seed/work flows.
